name: Corn Seed Yield
description: Corn yield (lbs/acre) from regular vs kiln-dried seed on 11 paired plots.
studyDescription: An agricultural experiment compared corn yields from regular seed versus kiln-dried seed. Each of 11 plots was split in half — one half planted with regular seed, the other with kiln-dried seed. This paired (split-plot) design controls for soil quality and other plot-level variation.

variableDescriptions.regular: Corn yield in pounds per acre from the half of the plot planted with regular (untreated) seed.
variableDescriptions.kiln_dried: Corn yield in pounds per acre from the half of the plot planted with kiln-dried seed.

sourceDetail: Snedecor, G.W. and Cochran, W.G. Statistical Methods, 8th ed. Iowa State University Press. A classic example of a paired experimental design in agricultural research.

context.nullClaim: no difference in yield between regular and kiln-dried seed
